pattern: Using t numbers each ending in k contributes a units digit of t*k mod 10, so the smallest t whose multiple of k matches num's last digit and stays within num is the answer.
hints: If you use t numbers all ending in k, what does the units digit of their sum have to be?
The total's last digit is (t * k) mod 10, and t numbers ending in k sum to at least t * k.
Try t from 1 upward (at most 10); the first t where t*k <= num and (t*k) mod 10 == num mod 10 works, otherwise it is impossible (and num == 0 needs zero numbers).

edge_inputs.description: impossible to reach this units digit
k is zero but num is non-zero is impossible
k zero and num zero needs nothing
num itself ends in k, single number
k larger than num cannot be formed

stress.description: all num/k combinations across the valid range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num = rng.randrange(0, 3001)
    k = rng.randrange(0, 10)
    return [num, k]
stress.note: tiny inputs, exercises feasibility edge cases
